title: Auth pipeline plugs: ExtractApiKey, ResolveApiKey, SetTenant, RequireAuth

story.i_want_to: implement a chain of Plug modules — ExtractApiKey (reads Bearer token from Authorization header), ResolveApiKey (SHA-256 hash lookup), SetTenant (sets RLS context via SET LOCAL), and RequireAuth (halts unauthenticated requests) — that together form the authentication pipeline
story.so_that: every authenticated API request goes through a consistent pipeline that extracts credentials, verifies them, sets the tenant context for RLS, and rejects unauthorized access

rationale: The auth pipeline is the gateway for every API request. Separating concerns into individual plugs makes each step independently testable and composable. ExtractApiKey is pure header parsing, ResolveApiKey does the cryptographic verification, SetTenant bridges to the RLS layer, and RequireAuth is the final guard. This matches the pipeline described in PRD section 4.2.

acceptance_criteria.description: LoopctlWeb.Plugs.ExtractApiKey reads the Authorization header, expects 'Bearer <token>' format, and assigns :raw_api_key to conn.assigns
ExtractApiKey passes through without error if no Authorization header is present (downstream plugs handle the missing key)
LoopctlWeb.Plugs.ResolveApiKey takes :raw_api_key from conn.assigns, calls Auth.verify_api_key/1, and on success assigns :current_api_key and :current_tenant to conn.assigns
ResolveApiKey skips verification if :raw_api_key is nil (no header was provided)
LoopctlWeb.Plugs.SetTenant reads :current_api_key from conn.assigns and calls Repo.put_tenant_id/1 with the key's tenant_id to set the RLS context for the current process
SetTenant sets RLS bypass mode when the API key role is :superadmin (tenant_id is nil)
LoopctlWeb.Plugs.RequireAuth halts the connection with 401 JSON error if :current_api_key is not set in conn.assigns
The plugs are composed into an :authenticated pipeline in the router that can be applied to API scopes
Suspended tenants (status != :active) are rejected with 403 at the ResolveApiKey step

test_cases.name: ExtractApiKey extracts Bearer token from header
steps: Build a conn with Authorization header 'Bearer lc_test_key_123'

test_cases.name: ExtractApiKey handles missing Authorization header
steps: Build a conn without Authorization header

test_cases.name: ResolveApiKey resolves valid key and assigns tenant
steps: Build a conn with assigns.raw_api_key = raw_key

test_cases.name: ResolveApiKey rejects suspended tenant
steps: Build a conn with assigns.raw_api_key = raw_key
expected_results: Response body indicates tenant is suspended

test_cases.name: SetTenant sets RLS context for tenant-scoped key
steps: Build a conn with assigns.current_api_key = api_key
expected_results: RLS queries will be scoped to this tenant

test_cases.name: RequireAuth halts unauthenticated request
steps: Build a conn without :current_api_key in assigns
expected_results: Response body is JSON with error message

test_cases.name: Full pipeline authenticates and sets tenant context
steps: Send a request to an authenticated endpoint with Authorization: Bearer <raw_key>
expected_results: The controller has access to conn.assigns.current_api_key and conn.assigns.current_tenant

test_cases.name: Superadmin key sets RLS bypass
steps: Build a conn with assigns.current_api_key = superadmin api_key
expected_results: RLS bypass mode is enabled in the process dictionary
Subsequent queries can see all tenants' data

technical_notes: Each plug implements the Plug behaviour with init/1 and call/2
ExtractApiKey should handle malformed Authorization headers gracefully (not crash)
ResolveApiKey should preload the tenant association to avoid N+1 in downstream code
SetTenant bridges the Phoenix request lifecycle to the Ecto/RLS layer via the process dictionary
The pipeline order in the router: plug ExtractApiKey -> plug ResolveApiKey -> plug SetTenant -> plug RequireAuth
RequireAuth should use the FallbackController error format for consistency
Consider a test helper that builds an authenticated conn: build_conn() |> authenticate(api_key)

Testing infrastructure: (1) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (2) Default permissive stubs in DataCase/ConnCase setup via Mox.stub/3, (3) Mox.set_mox_from_context(tags) for async isolation, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex with build/2 (no DB) and fixture/2 (DB insert + auto-deps), (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests.
